name: Stoford
overview: Stoford is a small village in the civil parish of South Newton, on the A36 Salisbury-to-Warminster road on the left bank of the River Wylye, about 5 to 6 miles north-west of Salisbury. It sits opposite Great Wishford across the river, where an old ford - which gave the village its name - had been replaced by a bridge by the early 18th century. Historically Stoford was a line of farmsteads held of South Newton manor.
character: A compact riverside village along the old Warminster road, with the chalk-stream Wylye and its water meadows immediately to the west.
amenities: The Swan Inn, a historic riverside coaching inn with restaurant, skittle alley and fishing (check current opening, as it has periodically been closed)
St Andrew's parish church at neighbouring South Newton
Bus stop on the A36 with services to Salisbury and Wilton
No village shop - everyday shopping is in Wilton or Salisbury

commutes.notes: About 6 miles south-east via the A36; Wilton is closer
From Salisbury (about 6 miles) on South Western Railway
The A36 bus stops right outside the village

prices.summary: Stock is mainly riverside cottages and detached houses; positions overlooking the Wylye attract a premium, while proximity to the A36 is a factor for some homes. See the live Rightmove sold-price page for current figures.

thingsToDo: Fly-fishing and riverside walks along the River Wylye
Crossing to Great Wishford to explore the wider valley and downland
Day trips to Stonehenge and Wilton, both a short drive away
placesToEat: The Swan Inn, Stoford - riverside pub and restaurant
pros: Pretty riverside setting on the chalk-stream Wylye
Has its own historic village inn
Close to Salisbury, Wilton and Stonehenge
cons: Sits directly on the busy A36 trunk road
Very limited amenities and no village shop or school
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a small riverside Wylye-valley village with a pub and quick A36 access to Salisbury and Stonehenge.

sources.label: British History Online - VCH Wiltshire, South Newton (Stoford)
South Newton & Stoford Parish Council - the Swan Inn
